description: The selected ontology term represents a different entity from the one described in the BioSample metadata.
The selected entity is related to the sample, but it belongs to the wrong attribute type, such as disease versus tissue or cell line versus cell type.
The selected ontology term is an overly general ancestor or category for the intended entity.
The selected ontology term is an overly specific subtype or instance that is not supported by the metadata.
The selected term is biologically or experimentally related to the metadata but is not an equivalent annotation for the sample.
The selected term only covers part of a composite expression and loses important meaning from the original metadata.
The input text is ambiguous or too short, but the pipeline resolved it to a specific ontology term without enough evidence.
The pipeline treated negated, control, untreated, wild-type, none, or not-applicable metadata as a positive entity annotation.
The BioSample metadata does not provide enough evidence to support the selected mapping.
The error appears to be caused by a missing, obsolete, or version-mismatched ontology term, leading to a near but incorrect mapping.
The pipeline produced no final ontology mapping even though the BioSample metadata appears to contain a mappable entity for the evaluated attribute.
The error does not fit the other categories.
